title: Implement Page Object Model
description: Learn to implement the Page Object Model design pattern for maintainable test automation
instructions:
Create a Page Object Model structure:
1. Create a LoginPage class with locators and methods
2. Create a DashboardPage class
3. Write tests using these page objects
4. Implement method chaining for fluent API
5. Use proper TypeScript/class structure

        # TODO: Define locators
    
    # TODO: Implement login method
    
    # TODO: Implement other methods

    pass

solution.python:
# pages/login_page.py
class LoginPage:
    def __init__(self, page):
        self.page = page
        self.username_input = page.locator('#username')
        self.password_input = page.locator('#password')
        self.login_button = page.get_by_role('button', name='Login')
        self.error_message = page.locator('.error')
    
    def navigate(self):
        self.page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/login')
        return self
    
    def login(self, username, password):
        self.username_input.fill(username)
        self.password_input.fill(password)
        self.login_button.click()
        return self
    
    def get_error_message(self):
        return self.error_message.text_content()

# pages/dashboard_page.py
class DashboardPage:
    def __init__(self, page):
        self.page = page
        self.welcome_message = page.locator('.welcome')
        self.logout_button = page.get_by_role('button', name='Logout')
    
    def is_loaded(self):
        self.welcome_message.wait_for()
        return True
    
    def logout(self):
        self.logout_button.click()

# tests/test_login.py
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
from pages.login_page import LoginPage
from pages.dashboard_page import DashboardPage
import pytest

class TestLogin:
    def test_successful_login(self, page: Page):
        login_page = LoginPage(page)
        login_page.navigate()
        login_page.login('practice', 'SuperSecretPassword!')
        
        dashboard = DashboardPage(page)
        expect(dashboard.welcome_message).to_be_visible()
    
    def test_failed_login(self, page: Page):
        login_page = LoginPage(page)
        login_page.navigate()
        login_page.login('invalid', 'invalid')
        
        error = login_page.get_error_message()
        assert 'invalid' in error

hints: Create separate classes for each page
Methods should perform actions on the page
Keep business logic in tests, not in page objects
Use TypeScript for better IDE support

testCases.name: Locators properly defined
testCases.expectedBehavior: All page elements are defined as locators

testCases.name: Methods implemented
testCases.expectedBehavior: Page actions are encapsulated in methods
